title: wyred contract v0 — connector-pinout lock gate record (.connlock.json)
description: Descriptive schema for the connector-pinout lock-gate self-check, emitted for every emit that fired a group covering connector_pinout (mppt_2420_hc_reva, watchy_v1_reva, watchy_v1_revb). Source: wyred/emit.py connlock path + wyred/paths.py `check_connector_locks`. There is NO prose (F1). Eight keys always emitted (artifact/gate_clean/groups/ok/rows/tamper_net_codes/tamper_series_codes/unlocked_rows); `fork_vs_parent_codes` is OPTIONAL — present only on forks (watchy_v1_revb), where the emit re-verifies its rows against the PARENT baseline. The four code arrays hold connector-lock gate codes: see F12 for the closed-vocabulary decision (the arrays are enum-constrained to the two codes `check_connector_locks` can emit). additionalProperties:false.

groups.description: the fired lock-group names that cover the connector_pinout decision class (author-defined names, e.g. "external-interface").

rows.description: number of connector-pinout rows the emit produced

unlocked_rows.description: sorted "<connector>.<pin>" identifiers of any connector-pinout row that carries no `locked_by` (a gate-clean emit has none). Free strings; [] in every golden.

gate_clean.description: codes from validating the emit against its OWN retained baseline; [] means the gate is clean.

tamper_net_codes.description: codes the gate must raise when a locked row's net is rewritten under the same series (counter-probe): ["CONNECTOR_LOCK_VIOLATION"] in goldens.

tamper_series_codes.description: codes the gate must raise for a hand-edited series with no fork record (counter-probe): ["CONNECTOR_SERIES_UNJUSTIFIED"] in goldens.

fork_vs_parent_codes.description: OPTIONAL, forks only: codes from re-verifying the fork's rows against the PARENT's baseline; [] means the fork honors the parent lock (watchy_v1_revb).

ok.description: overall gate verdict for this emit

connector_lock_code_array.description: An array of connector-pinout lock-gate codes. F12: the vocabulary is CLOSED to the two codes `wyred/paths.py::check_connector_locks` can emit — CONNECTOR_LOCK_VIOLATION and CONNECTOR_SERIES_UNJUSTIFIED — the sole gate producer of every code in gate_clean/tamper_net_codes/tamper_series_codes/fork_vs_parent_codes. Duplicates are legal (the gate emits one code per violating row). The only out-of-vocabulary string any producer can inject is the emit.py sentinel "(no parent baseline)" into fork_vs_parent_codes, which appears in NO golden and only on a broken fork (ok=false); encoding it would be a silent widening (invariant 1), so it is excluded — a future connlock carrying it, or any third code, is a contract event.
